source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 144 | score=4] 3494c: Silvanesti Betrayed Flushed with victory, the dragonarmies turn south on a “routine patrol” and attack Silvanesti’s northern border. Losses are heavy on both sides. When the elves lure their foes into ambush, Takhisis commits the rest of her troops to the battle, ravaging the forest border. The elves retreat to their capital, Sil- vanost, where they stand a good chance of outlasting the siege. However, King Lorac Caladon of the Silvanesti has become enchanted by the Dragon Orb of Istar, an artifact he rescued from the lost city before the Cataclysm. When he orders the evacua- tion of Silvanesti, the elves flee in ships for Southern Ergoth and Qualinesti. As the dragonarmies reach the 
[PDF_PAGE 156 | score=4] Minor Dragon Realms Although the five chromatic dragon overlords control the largest regions of Ansalon, many smaller wyrms survived the Dragon Purge, too. Some of these dragon lords have entrenched them- selves in realms of their own. Brynseldimer, a Sea Dragon Dragons rule not only the land, but beneath the waves as well. The sea lord best known today is Brynseldimer, the enemy of the Dimernesti sea elves. The Dragon Lord Brine (sea dragon male wyrm, tyranni- cal demeanor) arrived at the undersea nation five hundred miles southeast of Ansalon less than five years ago and attacked the capital, Dimernost, with steam breath. The super-heated water killed the elves sent to confront him, as wel
[PDF_PAGE 28 | score=3] consider each of these races too easily distracted from the pursuit of science and technology. Centaurs and minotaurs remain incomprehensible to gnomes, both too rugged and too brutal for comfort. Fur- thermore, neither race is particularly noted for its craftsmanship. Requirements In recognition of gnomish cleverness, heroes must have Reason and Percep- tion scores of 6 at minimum to select this race. In addition, as gnomes are noted for their ability to do fine work, heroes of this race must have a mini- mum Dexterity score of 7. Physically, gnomes are both diminutive and weak. Therefore, all gnome heroes have maxi- mum scores of 4 in both Strength and Endurance. The gnomish physique makes
[PDF_PAGE 44 | score=3] Rewarding the heroes makes players eager to continue the adventure and encourages truly heroic behavior. Hero rewards can take many forms. Reputation Increases When a hero completes an adventure, he marks another quest on his hero sheet. Completing enough quests increases his reputation rating. Monetary Rewards Sometimes, adventures can reward heroes (especially those with a merce- nary bent) with great wealth. Perhaps a powerful figure offers to pay the heroes for accomplishing the goal of the adventure, such as freeing a town from the ravages of a young dragon. Heroes can turn a profit from quests in other ways, too. After all, dragons keep great hoards of treasure in their lairs: gold, si
[PDF_PAGE 139 | score=2] y. E ISN = А ay BEN = reclaim it. When the populace, terrified by the gem’s chaotic effects, threatens to kill all the Chosen for bringing the stone to Krynn, the Graygem trans- forms this group of them into Scions: silver-bearded, golden-eyed wielders of wild magic. 3100-2900»c: Kal-Thax Built. The Scions urge the rest of Reorx’s Chosen to leave their secluded north- ern home. In great barges, they all flee south toward Ansalon, though the Scions are hated and feared by even the other Chosen folk, because of their supernatural abilities. The long and difficult voyage takes its toll on the little fleet; upon making landfall at Nordmaar, the few surviving Chosen of Reorx feed most of the Scio
[PDF_PAGE 142 | score=2] 530-522рс: Kernen Skirmishes. Ogres from the new Khalkist nation of Kernen threaten trade routes, but Thoradin, Istar, and the Knights of Solamnia drive them back. Thoradin signs the Swordsheath Scroll, as well as the Dwarfmeld: an exclusive trade treaty with Istar. 490-476»c: Raiders/Greatmeld. Solamnia becomes more dependent on Istaran trade, currency, and ideals. Mean- while, barbarians from Khur, Nordmaar, of offenses on his list of Evil acts—the Proclamation of Manifest Virtue—face confiscation of their lands and prop- erty, slavery, execution, or the gladiato- rial arena. Priests of Istar begin losing their clerical magic as they drift away from the teachings of their gods and become m
[PDF_PAGE 150 | score=2] iIBeryllíntbranor the Green Beryllinthranox fought dozens of wyrms in the Dragon Purge to win Qualinesti. She desires to one day best Malystryx, but first must add to her power. Initially, she had hoped to build up an arsenal of magic by infusing the spirits of elves into a collection of nefarious implements. However, the Last Conclave recently foiled that plan (see Chapter One). Since then, Beryl has concentrated on expanding her domain to rival Malystryx’s in size, if not magic. Her influence now spreads from Qualinesti south to the Kharolis shore and as far east as Thorbardin and the edge of the frost-wight lands. All this she has transformed into woodlands through the dragon magic born t
[PDF_PAGE 158 | score=2] currents directed eastward), and the vigil over his land. The few daring mer- > vest: 5 Г: Ж зү esta, a Brass Dragon, and forestation efforts of Beryllinthranox. chants who manage to traffic their 27 S stcnndunuu: 6,1 Blu € Dragon Despite these transformations, wal- exotic pelts and cold-water fish across 273 9 rus men and ice bears still outnumber — theblues domain to the Missing City by ret [д SS << ES 272 Shocking as it may seem, one dragon realm is ruled by a metallic dragon lord. During the Dragon Purge, blue Stenndunuus fought many competitors for the vast eastern tract of the Plains of Dust. Just as he defeated what he believed was his last foe, brass Iyesta descended upon him, claimi
